items.prompt: Solve `x + 7 = 19` and show how each step preserves equality.
items.expected_evidence: describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Solve `4x = 28`, then check the solution by substitution.
items.expected_evidence: compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Match the equation `n - 5 = 12` to a balance or missing-value situation and solve it.
items.expected_evidence: compare distributions using both numerical summaries and distribution features
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compare distributions using both numerical summaries and distribution features with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why subtracting the same value from both sides of an equation preserves the solution set.
items.expected_evidence: describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables.

items.prompt: Explain what the variable represents and why inverse operations help isolate it.
items.expected_evidence: compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases.

items.prompt: A student solves `x + 5 = 13` by writing `x = 13 + 5`. Diagnose the first structural mistake. Focus on summary selection fragmentation.
items.expected_evidence: describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables.

items.prompt: A student solves `3x = 18` by dividing only the `x`. Explain what went wrong. Focus on distribution feature opacity.
items.expected_evidence: compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases.

items.prompt: A gym charges a $12 signup fee plus $5 per class. Write and solve an equation for the number of classes if the total was $32.
items.expected_evidence: describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A balance model has three equal bags and 6 loose counters on one side and 24 counters on the other. Write and solve the equation.
items.expected_evidence: compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves compute and interpret mean, median, range, interquartile range, and mean absolute deviation in bounded cases in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Two weeks later, solve `m/3 = 8` and explain how you know the result is reasonable.
items.expected_evidence: describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates describe the shape and variability of bounded data distributions from plots or tables on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Before solving statistical distributions and variability, complete a prerequisite check on line plots and measurement data and explain how it supports the current equation work.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether line plots and measurement data is secure enough to support statistical distributions and variability
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in line plots and measurement data from a current-node issue in statistical distributions and variability.

items.prompt: Use a quick fraction benchmark estimation check to explain whether the learner is blocked by prerequisite structure or by statistical distributions and variability itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether fraction benchmark estimation is secure enough to support statistical distributions and variability
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in fraction benchmark estimation from a current-node issue in statistical distributions and variability.
